9.reason: Error: Loop condition should be i < len, not i <= len, to avoid accessing out-of-bounds when i equals len.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 0.5 , reason:  Error: Loop condition should be i < len, not i <= len, to avoid accessing out-of-bounds when i equals len.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 0.5 , reason:  Error: Loop condition should be i < len, not i <= len, to avoid accessing out-of-bounds when i equals len.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.5 , reason:  Error: loop should be i < len; otherwise accesses line[len] (null) and line[len+1] out of bounds, and when i=0, line[i-1] is undefined.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.5 , reason:  Loop includes null terminator; should be i < len to avoid printing '\0' and out-of-bounds access.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Loop condition i <= len accesses out-of-bounds index len (null terminator), should be i < len.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.25 , reason:  Loop bound i <= len causes out-of-bounds access when evaluating line[i+1] at i = len-1; should be i < len.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Loop runs to 'i <= len', which accesses line[len] (null terminator) and potentially line[len+1] in expansion logic, causing out-of-bounds access]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.25 , reason:  [Loop runs to 'i <= len', which accesses line[len] (null terminator) and potentially line[len+1] in the '-' handling logic, causing out-of-bounds read. Should be 'i < len'.]

13.reason: Error: No check for valid range or same type of characters around '-', and no check that line[i-1] < line[i+1]; also, when i is 0, line[i-1] accesses out-of-bounds.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 0.5 , reason:  Error: No check for valid range or same type of characters around '-', and no check that line[i-1] < line[i+1]; also, when i is 0, line[i-1] accesses out-of-bounds.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 0.5 , reason:  Error: No check that both sides of '-' are valid characters of the same type and that the right character is greater than the left; also missing handling for cases where expansion should not occur (e.g., different types or left >= right).
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.5 , reason:  Error: no validation that characters exist and are of same type (digit, uppercase, lowercase) with c[i-1] < c[i+1]; also fails to output '-' when expansion condition not met.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.5 , reason:  Multiple issues: no bounds check (i-1 or i+1 may be invalid), no validation of character types/ranges, no condition that expansion is allowed, and '-' is not printed when expansion is invalid. Also, loop may produce incorrect characters.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  1. Accesses line[i-1] out of bounds when i=0. 2. Lacks validation for character types and order (line[i-1] < line[i+1]). 3. Fails to print '-' when expansion conditions are not met.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.25 , reason:  Fails to verify that line[i-1] and line[i+1] are of the same type and that line[i-1] < line[i+1] as required; unconditionally expands and accesses invalid memory if i=0.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Does not validate that '-' is between valid same-type characters (e.g., a-z, A-Z, 0-9), nor checks bounds (i-1 >= 0, i+1 < len), leading to undefined behavior and incorrect output for invalid cases]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.25 , reason:  [No validation that '-' is between valid same-type characters (e.g., a-z, A-Z, 0-9), or that line[i-1] < line[i+1]. Also, accesses line[i+1] even when i == len (out-of-bounds). Additionally, uses 'char' for loop counter which may overflow.]

7.reason: gets is deprecated and removed in C11, causing compilation errors in modern environments; use fgets instead.

reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  gets is deprecated and removed in C11, causing compilation errors in modern environments; use fgets instead.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.25 , reason:  gets() is removed in the C11 standard and causes a compilation error in modern environments; also unsafe due to buffer overflow risk.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Using 'gets' is unsafe and deprecated; may cause compilation error with modern compilers due to removal from C standard]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.25 , reason:  [The function 'gets' is unsafe and deprecated; it was removed in C11. Use 'fgets' instead. Also, may cause buffer overflow if input exceeds array size minus one.]

11.reason: Missing boundary checks for i > 0 and i < len-1, leading to undefined behavior when accessing line[i-1] or line[i+1].

reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.25 , reason:  Missing boundary checks for i > 0 and i < len-1, leading to undefined behavior when accessing line[i-1] or line[i+1].

5.reason: [Using 'char' for loop variable 'k' can cause infinite loops or undefined behavior when incrementing beyond 127 (signed char overflow) or when dealing with values near CHAR_MAX. Should be 'int'.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.25 , reason:  [Using 'char' for loop variable 'k' can cause infinite loops or undefined behavior when incrementing beyond 127 (signed char overflow) or when dealing with values near CHAR_MAX. Should be 'int'.]
